  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX History (French Revolution, Russian Revolution, Hitler)",
      "source_text": "Outline political map of France/World. Locate/label/ identify Bordeaux, Nantes, Paris, Marseille; Central Powers: Germany, Austria-Hungary, Turkey; Allied Powers – France, England, Russia and USA",
      "related_unit": "Events and Processes",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter-1 The French Revolution"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography (India Size, Physical Features)",
      "source_text": "Locate/label: India – States and Capitals; Tropic of Cancer, Standard Meridian; Neighbouring Countries; Mountain Ranges; Peaks; Plateaus; Coastal Plains",
      "related_unit": "Physical and Human Geography of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 1 India – Size and Location"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography (Drainage, Climate)",
      "source_text": "Rivers: Indus, Ganges & Sutlej; Peninsular Rivers; Lakes. Annual rainfall in India, Monsoon wind direction",
      "related_unit": "Physical and Human Geography of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 3 Drainage"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class IX Geography (Population)",
      "source_text": "Population density of all states; The state having highest and lowest density of population",
      "related_unit": "Physical and Human Geography of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter-6. Population"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X History (Nationalism in India)",
      "source_text": "Congress sessions: 1920 Calcutta, Nagpur, Madras; Satyagraha movements: Kheda, Champaran, Ahmedabad mill workers; Jallianwala Bagh; Dandi March",
      "related_unit": "Events and Processes",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter II Nationalism in India"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Resources)",
      "source_text": "Major Soil Types, Major areas of Rice and Wheat; Producer states for Sugarcane, Tea, Coffee, Rubber, Cotton, Jute",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 1 Resources and Development"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Water Resources)",
      "source_text": "Locating: Salal, Bhakra Nangal, Tehri, Rana Pratap Sagar, Sardar Sarovar, Hirakund, Nagarjun Sagar, Tungabhadra",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 3 Water resources"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Minerals & Energy)",
      "source_text": "Iron Ore Mines: Mayurbhanj, Durg, Bailadila, Bellary, Kudremukh; Coal Mines: Raniganj, Bokaro, Talcher, Neyveli; Oil Fields: Digboi, Naharkatia, Mumbai High, Bassien, Kalol Ankaleshwar",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 5 Minerals and Energy Resources"
    },
    {
      "title": "Map Work - Class X Geography (Manufacturing & Lifelines)",
      "source_text": "Cotton textile Industries; Iron and Steel Plants; Software technology Parks; Major Sea Ports; International Airports",
      "related_unit": "Resources and Development of India",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 6 Manufacturing Industries"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities (Class X)",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Subject Enrichment Activity (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Project work (Interdisciplinary)-Disaster Management",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Disaster Management",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives include creating awareness, preparing for situations, and enhancing Life Skills.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project: Forest Society & Natural Vegetation",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with Chapter 4 of History 'Forest, Society and Colonialism' and Chapter 5 of Geography 'Natural Vegetation and Wildlife'. Compare forest situations in pre-colonial, colonial, post-colonial eras.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods, Economies and Societies",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter IV Forest Society and Colonialism"
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Consumer Rights / Social Issues / Sustainable Development",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project: Globalisation and Lifelines of Economy",
      "source_text": "Interdisciplinary project with History Chapter III 'The Making of a Global World', Geography Chapter 7 'Lifelines of National Economy', Economics Chapter 4 'Globalisation and the Indian Economy'. Analyse implications of globalisation, transportation as lifeline.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods, Economies and Societies / Everyday Life",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter III The Making of a Global World"
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class IX Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Class X History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class X Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class X Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class X Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment Class IX/X: 20 Marks total (Periodic Test 5, Multiple Assessment 5, Subject Enrichment/Project Work 5, Portfolio 5)",
      "Board Exam Question Paper Design: MCQs 20 marks, Long Answer Questions 43 marks, Case Study 12 marks, Map Pointing 5 marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Chapter 5 Pastoralists in the Modern World (Class IX History) - assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur (Class IX Economics) - assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter IV Climate Natural Vegetation and Wildlife (Class IX Geography) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in the annual examination",
      "Chapter IV The Age of Industrialisation (Class X History) - assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter 7 Lifelines of National Economy (Class X Geography) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in the Board Examination"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September. Carryover of project to home is strictly avoided.",
      "Projects should use eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.",
      "Project records (internal assessment) should be kept for three months from the date of declaration of result for verification.",
      "For Class IX, Project Work on Disaster Management requires collaboration with local authorities like Disaster Management Authorities, Fire Service, Police, etc.",
      "Map work can use maps available in the website of Govt. of India.",
      "Assessment includes self-assessment and peer assessment as per NEP-2020 guidelines."
    ]
  }
}
